tweets.text:
This take is largely confused a bit gay but it does capture the essence of a important reframe on introversion that few understand:

It’s not that extroverts are designed to socialize and introverts aren’t. It’s that extroverts are designed for social breadth and introverts for social depth. 

No one is happy isolated and alone. But extroverts can be happy with many shallow and temporary relationships in a way introverts cant. Introverts require a few very deep, relationship where they can be unapologetically themselves. 

They only need a handful. but without them, they too become depressed, lost, and confused.
tweets.name: Max

tweets.text:
it's almost always like this - people

they hate what makes them feel smaller

only the truly mature and developed can appreciate others without the snapping and biting voices of envy and jealousy filling their mind

i learned it from a young age, i've gone through it with many friends - some never reconciled again, some made stronger afterwards

and i go through it now on X all the time i see it

i pretend i don't, but it's always so glaringly obvious

they snap, they bite - they subtweet and throw their shade, they spread rumors, they do what they can to undermine me every step of the way

all while silently studying me, integrating me, wearing me like a cloak, taking my content, my mannerisms, my philosophy, my ideas - then repackaging and presenting them as their own

they'll cope and convince themselves that's not what they're doing, they'll tell themselves - "it was always inside me all along, i just first realized it through him" and similar solipsistic platitudes so that they can bear the weight of their own inferiority

sometimes i shit it all up because of people like that

i deliberately shit post and poison my timeline to sting their minds with venom

only issue is it can also consume me and i'll get possessed by my own tricks and forget that i also poison everyone else around me - like a social chemo attempting to kill cancerous cells but poisoning the entire social body in the process

with time i've learned that there is unfortunately no cure for such people

the only cure is to ignore and outgrow them

nothing else can be done that doesn't also harm oneself in the process

tweets.text:
(to spell it out for newcomers like @zby - the meta here is that we've spent the last two years discussing these rules, but instead of asking newcomers to read the archives and catch up, we're starting fresh. You don't need to catch up to anything, just join the round that's currently active 

And if you have good ideas about what the rules are or how they should work, you have a chance to contribute. To own a piece of it. To make it yours

The reason historically that fans of the Defender/ORI project liked it is that there's no sense of "inner circle". An outsider who just showed up with a good idea or question may influence something very core and fundamental on day 1. This kind of influence is unfakeable. It's not a marketing thing. You are encouraged to believe it only if YOU in particular can show up and do that, and it works. My job is to make this game/project able to take contributions from as many people as possible. And right now it's creaking under the weight of its currently popularity)
